steps: Sign in and open Google's “Results about you” tool.
Submit the URLs of search results that show your former name.
Pick the reason — use the personal-info/doxxing option for contact details, or the “outdated content” tool once the source page itself has changed.
Track each request's status in the tool; Google emails you the outcome.
deadnameRemoval.limits: Google can drop a result from its index, but the page still exists on the original site — opt out or request removal at the source too. Legitimate news and public-record pages are often refused.
deadnameRemoval.escalation: If a result is refused and it puts you at risk, re-submit citing personal safety, and pursue removal directly at the source site.

steps: Open the “Results about you” dashboard and turn on alerts so new results for your name and former name surface early.
In your Google Account → Data & privacy, turn off Web & App Activity if you don't want it retained.
Set your Google profile / About-me name to your current name.
